diagnoses.rationale: white-college D-share is over-predicting by 20.0pp. Survey-item-to-PRISM mapping for the nodes that distinguish this subgroup may be miscalibrated. This is a known realignment cell; check moral-boundary loading and culturally-coded items.
Democratic candidate signature may be off — check this candidate's node coding for the dimensions most salient to white-college.
white-college is a realignment cell. Inspect how moral-boundary salience and the salience-power weighting interact for this subgroup's dominant moral-boundary loadings.

recommendedInspection: Open mapping/survey-to-prism.yaml; review survey items that drive the nodes most salient to "white-college" (race / education / age axes for political-coalition shifts).
Cross-check moral-boundary loadings for white-college — uniform-mapper outputs across realignment cells often hide a load-bearing miscalibration.
Open src/historical/candidates.ts for the relevant election; review the Democratic candidate's node coding (especially MAT / CD / CU / ONT_S and AES if style was decisive).
Compare this candidate's signature against any nearby-year candidate to spot inconsistent coding.

Trace a single agent in this subgroup through respondentVoteChoice with telemetry on; see which node-impact term dominates the distance and whether that aligns with the subgroup's actual decisive axis.

topLevelHypotheses: No systematic cross-failure pattern detected. Each failure row stands on its own; treat per-row diagnoses as the working theory.
